metadata.title:
BARROW PLEADS FOR
' THE LIFE OF HAYWOOD

CALLS HAWLEY A LIAR AND
FiERCELY SCORES ORCH.
ARDS TESTIMONY AND
CHARACTER.

pages.raw_text:
BOISE, July 24.-"We will have
to leave to you, eventually, the in
terests Of these defendants, and 1t
rests with you to see that every
argument made against this man
and his fellows rests absolutely on
sound evidence. 1t is not Hay
woods fate that is In the balance;
1t is the fate of great worldwide
movement and we must ask you to
carefully examine every bit Of evil
dence when you make up your
minds"


In these words Attorney Darrow
this morning began his supreme Eff
fort to save the life of Haywood
and his colleagues. Continuing, he
said:


For more than a year and half
this community has been poisoned
by the lies Of the newspapers,
which engendered hatred and
created sentiment against this de-
PENDANT. This jury has been fed
upon such stories and naturally
must be hostile, but we believe you
will give this man fair treatment
and weigh carefully all evidence
against him.


"Why, gentlemen, nowhere in the
civilized world would any lawyer
attempt to try case on such
weak evidence as has been intro
duced in this case were he not re-
lying upon hostile sentiments.


Haywood Is charged with the
murder Of Steunenberg. He wasn't
In the state when that crime was
committed. There might be some
member of this jury who would
hesitate to take this mans life on
Orchards perjured word, but who
might have a lurking suspicion
that Haywood knew something
about it and want to send him to
the penitentiary.


Don't do that. Haywood Is not
a coward. Don't you be the cow
ard. II he is guilty, hang him by
the neck until dead, but If he is in.
nocent, release him. Don't, for
Gods sake, compromise"


barrow bitterly attacked Haw-
lay, intimating that he believed
him insane. He declared Hawley
lied when he said there was enough
evidence in the case to hang Hay
wood IL the evidence of Orchard
we're left out entirely.


He also declared Hawley lied
labor unions. .,


Haywood's invalid wife, sitting in
pillow lined chair by his side,
watched Darrow's every gesture
with bright eyes, seemingly forget.
ting her ailments in the struggle
for her husbands life, Only once
did she betray emotion, and that
was when Darrow declared Hay
wood would not finch, but would
stand with the rope around his
neck fearlessly as he ever faced
any situation that arose in his
past


"Hawley said everybody lied but
Orchard. and he insisted all other
witnesses were perjurers." con-
tinued Darrow. 81 think he is suf-
fering from Orcharditis. He, the
friend OF labor! God forbid! He,
the hired man of the state and
wants to hang another scalp, that
of BIZ Haywood, on his belt, to
honor his declining years" OAR
row defended the testimony of
Davis and Easterly.


Remember one thing" he said,
"Davis was safe In Nevada, but he
read Orchards damnable story and
so came over and placed his neck
in the moose and gave the another
end tot Hawley. to say Orchard
lied. don't care whether Davis
was at the Bunker Hill mill or not
There is not of you IL men who
would refuse to take the hand of
Davis, and you would all refuse to
